content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents can prioritize which episodic memories to compress and retain long-term by using their intrinsic curiosity signal as a weighting heuristic: only memories that still retain high prediction error (and thus unlearned informational value) are preserved in summarized form, while low-curiosity, predictable memories are discarded entirely rather than wasting compression budget on them. This creates a self-sustaining cycle where an agent’s intrinsic drive to explore automatically optimizes its long-term memory storage efficiency.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
